description: buildUserClaimsDict creates the dict with Object.create(null) (prototype-free) for user-controlled keys; the other two builders use plain {} which inherits Object.prototype. Unifying onto a shared {} builder re-introduces prototype-pollution writes on the auth path.

findings.rationale: buildUserClaimsDict initialises the dict via Object.create(null) — a CALL — while the other two use {} which is an object-literal. Same loop body, divergent DECL initialiser. Structurally a CALL vs OBJECT mismatch; the detector may classify it as label-divergence or structural-unclassified.
